question: what is pose capture used for?
answer: it validates your body posture before saving photos for ai cloth size prediction. it ensures full-body visibility inside a stand zone.

question: how many body points does it detect?
answer: 33 body keypoints landmarks including nose, shoulders, elbows, wrists, hips, knees, and ankles.

question: what visibility score is required?
answer: each landmark needs visibility 0.65. the system requires at least 30 out of 33 landmarks meeting this threshold.

question: does it use custom ai training?
answer: no. it uses google mediapipe blazepose pretrained model with model_complexity 1. no custom neural training is performed.

question: what are the api endpoints for pose capture?
answer: get for the auto-capture spa, post analyze to upload and analyze images, get captured file to serve saved captures.

question: how does size prediction work?
answer: you answer 10 questions age, height, weight, activity, body type, style preferences and ai predicts 25 kameez body measurements in inches.

question: what is the accuracy of size prediction?
answer: best accuracy is 81.6 within 1 inch using bert-base model. random forest is used in production with mean r² 0.584.

question: which measurements are predicted most accurately?
answer: sleeve length r² 0.972 , kameez length r² 0.883 , chest circumference r² 0.876 , and bicep r² 0.840 .

question: which measurements are hard to predict?
answer: shoulder slope r² 0.000 , front neck depth r² 0.000 , back neck depth r² 0.000 - these are weak targets.

question: what model is used in production?
answer: random forest because it is faster and has smaller model size 50-300 mb . the api rejects models above 800mb.

question: how is the dataset split for training?
answer: 70 training 34,999 rows , 15 validation 7,501 rows , 15 test 7,500 rows with random seed 42.

question: what are the 10 input questions?
answer: age, height, weight, activity level, body type, collar fit, shoulder type, fit preference, sleeve style, kameez length preference.

question: what chest size equals which garment size?
answer: chest 36 inches s, 36-39.9 m, 40-43.9 l, 44 xl

question: how does the chatbot work?
answer: it uses tf-idf to match your question to faq answers. if no match threshold 0.28 , it uses rag with faiss groq llm.

question: what models does the chatbot use?
answer: 12 models total tf-idf, cosine similarity, sentence-transformers, groq llama, mistral-7b, faiss, k-nn, chest-size rules, keyword filters, langchain, text splitter, pdf loader.

question: can the chatbot show me 3d products?
answer: yes! ask show me black kurta or recommend 3d products and it will return gltf urls for 3d viewing.

question: what is the similarity threshold for matching?
answer: 0.28 28 . if tf-idf similarity is above this, it answers from training csv. below this, it uses rag fallback.

question: how is the faiss memory built?
answer: it scans all pdfs in data folder, splits into 500-character chunks with 50 overlap, embeds with all-minilm-l6-v2, saves to vectorstore db_faiss

question: How do I try on a kurta virtually?
answer: Upload your portrait photo, select a kurta from 31 designs, then click Run Try-On. The AI will fit the kurta on your upper body using IDM-VTON model on Hugging Face.

question: What file format should my photo be?
answer: JPG or PNG format, portrait style, front view of your face and upper body.

question: How many kurta designs are available?
answer: 31 different kurta designs, all credited to affordable.pk

question: How long does the try-on take?
answer: 1-5 minutes queue time on Hugging Face Space, plus processing time. You will see a progress bar from 0-100%.

question: Can I download the result?
answer: Yes, after the try-on completes, you can view the result and download the picture.

question: What is pose capture used for?
answer: It validates your body posture before saving photos for AI cloth size prediction. It ensures full-body visibility inside a stand zone.

question: How many body points does it detect?
answer: 33 body keypoints (landmarks) including nose, shoulders, elbows, wrists, hips, knees, and ankles.

question: What visibility score is required?
answer: Each landmark needs visibility >= 0.65. The system requires at least 30 out of 33 landmarks meeting this threshold.

question: Does it use custom AI training?
answer: No. It uses Google MediaPipe BlazePose pretrained model with model_complexity=1. No custom neural training is performed.

question: What are the API endpoints for pose capture?
answer: GET / for the auto-capture SPA, POST /analyze to upload and analyze images, GET /captured/<file> to serve saved captures.

question: How does size prediction work?
answer: You answer 10 questions (age, height, weight, activity, body type, style preferences) and AI predicts 25 kameez body measurements in inches.

question: What is the accuracy of size prediction?
answer: Best accuracy is 81.6% within 1 inch using BERT-base model. Random Forest is used in production with mean R² = 0.584.

question: Which measurements are predicted most accurately?
answer: Sleeve length (R²=0.972), kameez length (R²=0.883), chest circumference (R²=0.876), and bicep (R²=0.840).

question: Which measurements are hard to predict?
answer: Shoulder slope (R²=0.000), front neck depth (R²=0.000), back neck depth (R²=0.000) - these are weak targets.

question: What model is used in production?
answer: Random Forest because it is faster and has smaller model size (50-300 MB). The API rejects models above 800MB.

question: How is the dataset split for training?
answer: 70% training (34,999 rows), 15% validation (7,501 rows), 15% test (7,500 rows) with random seed 42.

question: What are the 10 input questions?
answer: Age, height, weight, activity level, body type, collar fit, shoulder type, fit preference, sleeve style, kameez length preference.

question: What chest size equals which garment size?
How does the chatbot work?
answer: It uses TF-IDF to match your question to FAQ answers. If no match (threshold 0.28), it uses RAG with FAISS + Groq LLM.

question: What models does the chatbot use?
answer: 12 models total: TF-IDF, Cosine similarity, sentence-transformers, Groq Llama, Mistral-7B, FAISS, k-NN, chest-size rules, keyword filters, LangChain, text splitter, PDF loader.

question: Can the chatbot show me 3D products?
answer: Yes! Ask show me black kurta or recommend 3D products and it will return GLTF URLs for 3D viewing.

question: What is the similarity threshold for matching?
answer: 0.28 (28%). If TF-IDF similarity is above this, it answers from training CSV. Below this, it uses RAG fallback.

question: How is the FAISS memory built?
answer: It scans all PDFs in data/ folder, splits into 500-character chunks with 50 overlap, embeds with all-MiniLM-L6-v2, saves to vectorstore/db_faiss/

question: What happens if I ask about non-clothing items?
answer: It refuses and shows suggestion buttons for clothing-related topics only.

question: What are the two order types?
answer: Standard (seller ships directly to customer) and Custom (seller sends fabric to tailor, tailor stitches then ships).

question: How many status steps for custom orders?
answer: 9 steps: pending, withSeller, shippedToTailor, tailorDelivered, tailorStitched, tailorToShip, shipped, delivered, paymentReceived

question: How many steps for standard orders?
answer: 4 steps in circular tracking (simplified pipeline).

question: What technology handles order tracking?
answer: React 18 frontend, Firebase Auth + Cloud Firestore backend (project websmart-702de)

question: Can customers track by order ID?
answer: In production, it uses authenticated list-based tracking filtered by user ID. No public enter tracking ID field in current UI.

question: What are the main routes for tracking?
answer: /user/orders for customers, /seller/orders for sellers, /tailor/orders for tailors

question: What is the overall tech stack?
answer: React/Vite frontend, FastAPI/Flask backend, Hugging Face for AI models, Firebase for orders, MediaPipe for pose.

question: Where is the app deployed?
answer: Vercel for UI, Render for API, Hugging Face Spaces for IDM-VTON model.

question: How do I run the project locally?
answer: Prerequisites: Node 18+, Python 3.10+. Then run the frontend and backend servers as shown in the docs.

question: Who created SmartFitao?
answer: Asif Farooq (Researcher), Nauman Irshad Ali Shah, Umer Amir, Ali Ahmad, and Abdul Rehman from University of Central Punjab, Lahore.

question: What is SmartFitao?
answer: A smart tailoring platform for Pakistani kurta and shalwar kameez with virtual try-on, pose capture, size prediction, chatbot, and order tracking.
